failure_class: test_infrastructure -- HuggingChat OAuth session absent in test PC Chrome (all profiles checked)
status_detail: Cycle 95 cleanup A4 regression for huggingface NOT EXERCISED across 3 attempts. Engine intercept never reached. Root cause is auth — not automation. Verified across CDP attempt 1 (separate profile, /login redirect), CDP attempt 2 (same separate profile, SvelteKit hydration race redirected to /login mid-DOM-op), and windows-mcp attempt 3 (user's real Chrome window — document.cookie shows only stripe + aws-waf tokens, NO HuggingChat session cookie). The /chat/ page renders briefly then router.replace()s to /login because no OAuth token is present. windows-mcp itself worked correctly (Click/Type/Shortcut/Clipboard all succeeded, no focus-steal). The wall is at the cookie-jar layer, not the automation method. Resolution requires user to physically sign in to huggingface.co/chat once in this Chrome window via OAuth Authorize click, then re-dispatch the test.
verdict_subagent: PARTIAL (3rd attempt) — but engine A4 codepath untestable in current state

attempts_summary.outcome: FAIL — Start chatting modal redirected to /login

attempts_summary.method: CDP separate profile retry (modal-click + ESC + DOM-remove variants)
attempts_summary.outcome: FAIL — SvelteKit hydration race router.replace()'d to /login

attempts_summary.method: windows-mcp on user's real Chrome window (per user instruction)
attempts_summary.outcome: PARTIAL — automation worked (no focus steal), but Chrome cookies lack HuggingChat session; same /login redirect

intercept_endpoint: POST https://huggingface.co/chat/conversation/{uuid} (target — never reached in any attempt)
bypass_normal_answer: unknown (no POST sent in any attempt)
rst_stream_evidence: not generated -- no POST /conversation traffic to RST. Only Network.loadingFailed events were aborted /login OAuth redirects from the harness's own Page.navigate calls.
progress_vs_pre_cleanup: untestable -- gated by auth state, not engine behavior
regression_evidence: absence of evidence is not evidence of absence -- A4 regression cannot be ruled out from these runs; need re-test with valid HF session
automation_validated: windows-mcp pivot (attempt 3) confirmed: Click, Type, Shortcut (F12, Ctrl+V, Enter), Clipboard.SetText (Korean preserved), Console eval all functional. NO focus-steal observed. Lesson: windows-mcp is viable on this test PC when targeting user's real Chrome window directly (not a separate profile).
auth_diagnosis: document.cookie inspection on user's real Chrome at huggingface.co confirmed: only stripe_mid + stripe_sid + aws-waf-token cookies present. Missing: hf-* session cookies, HuggingChat OAuth bearer. User must click OAuth Authorize once after navigating to /chat/login.
errors_observed: DNS_PROBE_FINISHED_NXDOMAIN on stale 'data' tab (leftover from earlier CDP run, cosmetic)
SvelteKit router.replace() race during hydration (CDP attempt 2)

test_infra_findings.auth_state_gating: huggingface (and any login-gated service) requires valid OAuth session in user's actual Chrome window. CDP separate profiles do NOT inherit. Test PC profile state is not currently version-controlled.
test_infra_findings.windows_mcp_validated: windows-mcp on real Chrome works perfectly when prerequisites met (no focus-steal, no coordinate eyeballing failure). Earlier #638/#639 focus-steal symptom was likely caused by Chrome being unfocused initially — once explicitly foregrounded, windows-mcp drives it cleanly.
test_infra_findings.recommended_fix: Three options: (a) user manually clicks Authorize in OAuth flow once in test PC default Chrome, then re-dispatch #641; (b) cowork-remote runtime adds HF auth pre-check before scheduling huggingface check-warning runs; (c) engine team validates A4 via direct integration test against engine (no browser dep) for h2-framing-level regressions.

recommendation_for_dev: Re-dispatch #641 (or new ID) AFTER user completes OAuth Authorize in test PC Chrome at https://huggingface.co/chat/login
A4 regression check for huggingface remains OUTSTANDING — h2_hold_request=1 ignore behavior + delayed_ES + RST_STREAM still untested
Engine team can validate A4 alternatively via direct engine integration test (synthetic partial-body POST + RST_STREAM) — eliminates browser auth dependency for h2-framing regressions
Add 'auth_state_required' field to request envelopes so cowork-remote can pre-check before dispatching, surface NEEDS_USER_SESSION verdict early
notes: Cycle 95 A4 huggingface regression FAILED at auth gate across 3 attempts (CDP×2, windows-mcp×1). Engine codepath untested. windows-mcp itself validated as working on this test PC when targeting user's real Chrome. Resolution: user OAuth click required.
